To prepare the misl (of a case). misl murattab h., yā bannā , v. n. To be prepared (the whole proceedings of a case). misl-i-muqadma . The record or Misl of a case. misl meṅ shāmil, yā shāmil-i-misl k. , v. a. To file with the record; place on the record.

ety: v.n. of instr. fr. سطر 'to rule' (a book
def: s.f. An instrument with which a book is ruled (made of a piece of pasteboard with strings strained and glued across it, which is laid under the paper; the latter being ruled by being slightly pressed over each string); — a ruled line, a line.
more: n. m. سطر ruled. A pasteboard on which threads are strung parallel to each other and pressed against the sheet of paper to be written on, the native substitute for our black lines. mistar k. , v. a. To rule or make straight lines as above.

(noun of instrument of سطر Heb. and Chald. ) s. f. 1. A ruler (commonly, one made by stringing threads parallel to each other on paper, &c. and the lines are marked by pressing the sheet on this before writing). 2. A line.

def: adj. S. मिष्ट Opp. of kaṛvā bitter, namkīn saltish, khārī brackish, khaṭṭā sour, phīkā insipid. 1. Sweet. 1. Mīṭhī āvāz . A sweet voice. 2. Appan mīth, an-kar tīt . E. Prov. Your own is honey, another's gall! 3. Jo phal chakkhā nahīṅ, voh hi mīṭhā hai . Prov. Untasted fruit is sweetest. (The joy in prospect is sweeter than the joy possessed). 4. Jitnā guṛ ḍāloge utnā hī mīṭhā hogā . Prov. The more sweet you put in the sweeter it will be. (Good material, good work). 2. ( mīṭhā ) Slow; slight ( dhīmā , 3). 1. Mīṭhī mīṭhī āṅch hone do! 2. Miṭhā mīṭhā dard hotā hai . 3. Lazy; slow. Mīṭhā ghoṛā hai . 4. Cunning; crafty. Kaṛve se milye, mīṭhe se ḍarye! Prov. The bitter greet but fear the sweet! mīṭhā , n. m. 1. A general name for sweets, as sugar, molasses, etc. 2. See halvā , 1. 3. Sweet lime. mīṭhā baras , n. m. Sweet seventeen. (To say seventeen is held unlucky). Buā, Khudā rakkho! mere bachche ko ab mīṭhā baras lagā hai . Mah. Wom. mīṭhā bolnā , v. n. To speak mildly. mīṭhā pānī , n. m. 1. Sweet water. 2. ( Europeans ) Lemoeade. mīṭhā tambākū . Opp. of kaṛvā tamākū. mīṭhā tel , The oil of til , q. v., sweet oil. mīṭhā teliyā , A very active vegetable poison. mīṭhā ṭhag , n. m. A false friend; a traitor. mīṭhā ghīyā ; Tir. kaḍīmā , n. m. Red pumpkin or squash gourd; Cucurbita melopo . mīṭhā muṅh karānā , v. a. To give one sweetmeats, money, etc. mīṭhī bāt . Honeyed words. mīṭhī bolī , n. f. A soft language. Brij kī mīṭhī bolī hai. mīṭhī chhurī , n. f. Cold steel. mīṭhī gāliyāṅ , Poet. n. f. Sweet abuse. mīṭhī-mār , Slow torture. See qupat mār . mīṭhī nazar , n. f. Love glances. mīṭhī nazroṅ se dekhnā , v. n. To look sweet upon. mīṭhe-vāle , Thugs who kill travellers with a poison called mīṭhā-telyā. miṭh-lonā , adj. Sweet and saltish.

def: s.f. Measuring; measurement, measure; — weighing; weight; determinate amount; quantity; value; — evidence, proof.
more: n. f. 1. The day of the month (تاريخ 1). Mitī mitī kā biyāj chukā le! mūl bhī le kauḍī kauḍī! merī ṭūm gahanvā de, modī! Rus. Your interest take to date, with the principal, every penny. My jewels pawned, grocer, give back! 2. Interest; discount. mitī pūgnā , v. a. To mature (a bill). mitī chaṛhānā, yā ḍālnā , v. a. 1. See tārīkh chaṛhānā . 2. To finish a letter or writing. mitī-kāṭā , The rule for calculating discount. mitī kāṭnā , v. a. To discount. mitī-vār , adv. According to date ( tārīkh-vār ). pakkī mitī , n. f. Due date; maturity. Interest allowed by bankers on money received, dating from the day after the receipt. pahlī mitī chaṛhānā , v. a. To antedate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian متی (mattā), from Arabic مَتَّى (mattā). The shift from final -ā to -ī could be due to mispronunciation of the alif maqsura.

more: v. n. To be effaced, to expire.

def: s.f. Earth, soil, land, clay, loam; — dirt, filth; excrement; sweepings, rubbish, trash; — lifeless clay, corpse; carcass; — flesh, meat; — temperament, constitution
more: s. f. Earth. مٿي پر لڙنا maṭṭī par laṛnā, To dispute about land. مٿي پکڙنا miṭṭī pakaṛnā, 1. To bite the ground. 2. To be overcome, or overturned. 3. To be perverse, or obstinate, to resist. مٿي دينا maṭṭī denā, To bury. مٿي ڐالنا maṭṭī ḍālnā, To conceal the crime or fault of another. مٿي کرنا maṭṭī karnā, To ruin or destroy. مٿي مين ملنا maṭṭī meṅ milnā, To be ruined or disgraced. مٿي کهانا maṭṭī khānā, To eat flesh. مٿي هونا maṭṭī honā, To become weak or faint, to be ruined.

To seize or bite the ground; — to be laid low; to be overcome or overturned; — to be perverse or obstinate, to resist

(-kī), The dust or clay (of a person) to be defiled; the obsequies (of a person) to be marred or spoiled; — to be badly off; — to be treated with indignity; to be beaten severely; to be dragged through the mire
(-kī), The corpse to be carried to its resting-place; to be buried or interred; the funeral obsequies to be performed; 'to shuffle off this mortal coil'
s.m. Wretched condition, sad plight; — wreck, ruin, destruction; — disgrace, ignominy
or miṭṭī ḵẖẉār honā (-kī) = miṭṭī palīd honā, q.v.

s.m. The earthly tabernacle, the human frame, the body

adj. Of the price or value of dirt, cheap as dirt

v.n. To be levelled with the ground; to be marred or spoilt; to be ruined or disgraced
v.n. To become (or turn to) clay; — to become weak or faint; to become cold and insipid (as food); to be spoiled, or ruined, or destroyed (syn. ḵẖāk ho-jānā, q.v.)

The (surviving) representative (or the offspring) of deceased parents.

def: s.f. A worm generated in the intestines; the tape-worm; Ascarides.

def: s.m. The middle, centre; waist, loins; — a scabbard, sheath; — adv. Between, among, in the midst (of)
subs: adj. Loin-girded, with the loins girt, ready, prepared (for action)
s.m. Two men living with one woman
s.f. The middle fold (of any garment which consists of three folds)
adj. & s.m. Going between; — having a sheath, sheathed; (in Bot.) villous; — a middle-man; broker; mediator; procurator
s.m. A tract of land lying between two rivers (syn. antarbed)
To be out of the scabbard, to be unsheathed (a sword); — to pass all bounds, to go beyond the happy mean; to be transported with rage
v.t. To draw from the scabbard, to unsheathe (a sword)
v.t. To put into the scabbard, to sheathe (a sword).

ety: Prk. मिअओ, मित्तओ; S. मित्र+कः
def: s.m. An address expressive of kindness, or respect; Sir! good Sir! good man; master; husband; lord; father; a title by which eunuchs are addressed; — (in the hill-districts) title of the sons of Rājpūt princes; — an evil spirit
more: n. m. 1. Waist; loins. 2. A scabbard; sheath. 1. Miyān meṅ se niklī hī paṛe hai! He is all too ready to draw his sword. 2. Ek miyān meṅ do talvāreṅ nahīṅ rah saktīṅ . Prov. One scabbard cannot hold two swords. (Two men cannot have one woman between them). miyān bhāī , Two men living with one woman. miyān se bāhar h. , v. n. 1. To be unsheathed (a sword). 2. To be trausported with rage. miyān-duāb , n. m. A tract of land lying between two rivers ( antarbed ). miyān-tah , n. f. An under-lining. miyān-dār . ( Bot .) Sheathed; villous. miyān se kheṅchnā , v. a. To draw the sword. miyān meṅ k. , v. a. To sheathe the sword.
s. m. Sir! an address expressive of kindness, a title by which eunuchs are addressed, master. ميان آدمي miyāṅ-ādmī, A respectable person. ¶ myāṅ (dakh.) Scabbard: see نيام niyām.

s. f. m. 1. A scabbard. 2. The loins, the waist, the middle. ميان بسته miyān-basta, Ready, prepared (for action), loins-girt. ميان تـﮩي miyān-tah-ī, s. f. The middle fold of any garment which consists of three. ميان جي miyān-jī, s. m. A mediator, an arbitrator, a broker, a go-between, a schoolmaster. ميان دار miyān-dār, Mediator.
subs: s.m. A good-natured man; — a respectable person, a gentleman
s.m. A schoolmaster; the respected master (esp. schoolmaster); — a go-between (i.q. bhaṛwā)
s.f. The profession of schoolmaster; teaching.

def: Mah. n. m. Cor. of pl. mīrāṅ chiefs. 1. Sir! good Sir. Āīye, miyāṅ Sāhab! 2. Master; husband ( patī ). Miyāṅ āve dūroṅ se, ghoṛe-bāṅdhūṅ khajūroṅ se . Nursery rhyme. 3. Father; papa. 4. An evil spirit. Ek to miyāṅ kī the, dūje khāī bhāṅg . Prov. Already by the evil spirit possessed he makes himself with bhang intoxicate. 5. In the hill districts, the title of the sons of Rājput princes. mīyāṅ-ādmī , n. m. 1. A good-natured man. 2. A gentleman. mīyāṅ-jī , n. m. 1. A schoolmaster. 2. A go-between ( bhaṛvā ). mīyāṅ-jī-garī , n. f. 1. Teaching. 2. Pimping ( bhaṛvāī , 1).
ex: کْیا بات کَر رَہے ہو مِیاں؟
kyā bāt kar rahe ho miyā̃
What are you talking about, mister?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian میان (miyān, “center, (India) lord, sir”). First attested as Old Hindi मींयां (mī̃yā̃).

ety: rel. n. fr. miyān, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Middling; middle-sized; mean; moderate; — the middle, centre; middle space; interstice; — a middle-sized horse, a galloway; — pole (of a carriage, &c.); an axis; — a kind of sedan or pālkī (with curtains; i.q. menā, q.v.)
more: adj. Middling; moderate; middle-sized. miyān'ah , n. m. 1. A centre. 2. A pole (of a carriage). 3. A kind of sedan or pālkī . 4. A galloway. miyāna-ravī , n. f. Moderation (in expenditure). miyāna-qad , Of middle stature.

1. subst. Middle. 2. adj. Middling, moderate. 3. (In Hind.) s. m. A kind of sedan or pālkī. 4. (dakh.) A pole (of the earth), an axis ( Harris ). ميانه روي miyāna-rau-ī, s. f. Moderation. ميانه قد miyāna-ḳadd, Of middle stature.
subs: s.f. Moderation (in expenditure), economy; a middle course, the golden mean

n. f. The mewing of a cat. Hamārī hī billī, aur ham hī se miyāūṅ! Prov. My own cat, and spit at me!

def: s.m. Jesting with (another).

r: mizāj, lit., mizāj bigaṛnā, mizāj-pānā, mizāj pūćhnā, kā, mizāj-dār
ety: v.n. fr. مزج 'to mix'
def: adj. Proud, haughty, conceited
more: n. m. مزج mixed. 1. Nature; temperament. Is davā kā mizāj garm hai . 2. Temper; humour; disposition. 3. Pride; hauteur. 1. Tumhārā to mizāj hī nahīṅ pāyā jātā . 2. Ranḍī kā sā mizāj karte ho . You are showing all the airs of a harlot. mizāj bigaṛnā , v. n. To be spoiled (one's temper). mizāj pānā , v. a. To find one in good humour. Mizāj pāte haiṅ to kuchh kahte hain. mizāj pūchhnā , v. n. To inquire after one's health. mizāj-dār , adj. Conceited; proud. mizāj-dāṅ . Knowing the temperament or constitution. mizāj sharīf, mizāj mubārak . A respectful form of inquiry after one's health. mizāj k. , v. a. 1. To show temper. 2. To give oneself airs. Kyoṅ mīzāj karte ho? mizāj ke māre , Through pride or temper. mizāj meṅ ānā . v. n. 1. To like. 2. To be proud. mizāj na milnā , v. n. To be unable to understand or fathom one. mizāj'o, mizājan, mijājo, mijājan, mijāz-pīṭī , Wom. adj. Proud; haught…
(from مزج Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. 1. Temperament, constitution, complexion, habit of body. 2. Temper, humour, disposition. مزاج دان mizāj-dān, Knowing the temperament.
subs: adj. & s.m. Knowing (or one who knows) the temperament or constitution; — a confidant
(or mizāj-sharīf), or mizāj-ě-ʻālī, or mizāj-ě-mubārak, interrog. Your noble, or exalted, or blessed disposition or health (how is it?), how is your Honour's health? how do you do?
To show temper; — to give oneself airs
(-kā), To discover or understand the disposition or temper or humour (of); to understand or fathom (a person)
v.n. To fit in with the humour, to suit the temper or humour (of, -ke), to please; to like; — to stand upon (one's) dignity, to be proud
adj. Of a pleasant disposition; good-humoured
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مزاج (mizāj), from Arabic مِزَاج (mizāj, “temperament”).

def: adj. Constitutional; habitual; — humoursome; fastidious, squeamish, nice; — proud, haughty; — s.m. A humoursome, &c man; one who gives himself airs.

(noun of instrum. of وزن) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. A balance, a pair of scales. 2. The sign Libra. 3. Measure, metre, rhyme, verse, prosody. 4. Addition (in arithmetick). ميزان دينا يا لگانا mīzān-denā or -lagānā, To add, to cast up.
subs: or mīzān karnā, or mīzān lagānā (-kī), To add up, to total
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian میزان (mīzān), from Arabic مِيزَان (mīzān).

def: s. A turret or some elevated place from which people are called to prayer at a mosque.

s.f. Eyelid; — eyelash.

def: s.f. = next, q.v.

s.m. A foreigner, barbarian, non-Aryan; man of an outcast race; a generic term for a person speaking any language but Sanskrit or some language derived from it, and not conforming to the usual Hindū institutions; — a wicked or bad man, miscreant, sinner; an infidel; — an unclean person or race, one who makes (or those who make) no distinction between clean and unclean food; — a glutton
subs: or malećh bhāshā, s.f. A barbarous or foreign language; — a Hindī jargon containing a large admixture of Persian and Arabic
s.m. 'Food of foreigners or barbarians'; wheat; — half-ripe barley
vulg. malećh-jāt, s.m. & f. A person belonging to one of the barbarian races; one of an outcast race; a barbarian, a savage; a mountaineer (as a Kirāta, &c.)

vulg. malećh-des, s.m. A foreign or barbarous country; the countries bordering on India; any country inhabited by people of a language and institutions different from those of the Hindūs; — any country where the black antelope is not found
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Speaking a barbarous or foreign language; — one who speaks a barbarous dialect.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Spoken barbarously, or unintelligibly, or inaccurately; barbarous, foreign; — a foreign or barbarous language; ungrammatical speech.

def: s.m. = mahūʼā, q.v. s.m. The tree Bassia latifolia, and its flower (a spirituous liquor is distilled from the blossom; and oil is extracted from the nuts or seeds, which is used instead of butter, and to adulterate ghī)

Spirituous liquor distilled from the flower of the mahuʼā.

ety: act. part. of عبّد, ii of عبد; prob. formed fr. ʻibādat
def: s.m. A worshipper (i.q. ʻābid, q.v.). s.m. A server, worshipper, adorer (of God), a votary, a devotee; a recluse.
ex: بھگوان ہوتا تو اس معبد کے اندر ہوتا، دہلیز پر نہ بیٹھا کرتا۔
If there was a god, he would have been inside this temple, not lingering at the entrance.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَعْبَد (maʕbad).

def: s.m. An interpreter of dreams.

ety: fr. mǒʻabbir
def: s.f. Interpretation of dreams.

ety: for A. معافي mǒʻāfā, pass. part. of عافي 'to forgive, pardon,' &c., iii of عفا (for عفو
def: part. adj. Forgiven, pardoned, absolved, excused, condoned, remitted; spared; dispensed with; exempted (from); free (of); privileged; — rent-free (land)
more: adj. عفو 1. Exempted ( barī , 1). 2. Forgiven; pardoned; excused. Tāzīm-i-kārigarāṅ muāf! Prov. Players. Pray hold the artists (players) excused from showing respect! 3. Rent-free (land). muāf bil-shart , A conditional pardon. muāf k. , v. a. 1. To absolve; dispense with. 2. To pardon; excuse. 3. To remit. muāf mutlaq , A free pardon. muāf h. , v. n. To be pardoned, excused.
(part. act. III. of عفو prop. معافي) adj. Absolved, exempted, dispensed with, forgiven, pardoned, excused. معاف کرنا muʼāﻌf karnā, To pardon, forgive, excuse, remit, absolve.
subs: v.t. To absolve, pardon, forgive, excuse; to dispense with; to remit
v.n. To be forgiven, or pardoned, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُعَاف (mu'āf), borrowed from Arabic مُعَافًى (muʕāfan), from عَافَى (ʕāfā).

ety: mǒʻāf, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Released, exempted; exempt from the payment of rent or tax, rent-free; — s.f. Forgiveness, pardon, absolution, remission; exemption; — remission of revenue; — a rent-free grant; rent-free lands (syn. arāẓī-ě-mǒʻāfī); a grant of land from Government, an estate given as a pension
more: n. f. 1. ( muāfī ) A pardon; exemption. 2. Remission of revenue. 3. A rent-free grant; rent-free lands. 4. A grant from Government; an estate given as pension. muāfī bāqī , n. f. Remission of a balance. muāfī chāhnā , v. n. To beg or ask pardon or remission. muāfī chiṭṭhī , n. f. A free pass; exemption from payment of a toll or tax; a warrant of remission. It was formerly applied to certain classes of pilgrims, exempting them from payment of the pilgrim tax. muāfī hīn-hayāt , Released or exempted during life. muāfī-dār , n. m. A holder of rent-free land; a grantee. muāfī dāëmī, yā istamrārī , n. f. Released in perpetuity. muāfī-ravanna , n. m. An order or permit for the transit of merchandise free of duty. muāfī māṅgnā , v. n. To beg pardon; apologize. muāfī māmūlī , n. f. Established or customary remissions. muāfī nā-jāyaz , n. f. An illegal grant.

(part. act. III. of عفو or from معاف q. v.) adj. Exempt from rent and tax (land). s. f. A grant of land, &c. from a sovereign free of tax, immunity, exemption, remission, pardon, absolution. معافي نامه muʼāﻌfī-nāma, A deed of immunity or exemption from taxes, a deed of amnesty.
subs: (-kī), To ask or beg for pardon or remission
s.f. A letter or order for exemption (from payment of a toll, or tax, &c.); a warrant of remission; a free pass
adj. Released, or exempted, during life; — s.f. A grant of rent-free land for life
s.m. A holder of rent-free land, a grantee
or mǒʻāfī-ě-istimrārī, s.f. A grant of land in perpetuity; exemption (from tax, &c.) in perpetuity
s.m. An order, or a permit, for the transit of merchandise free of duty
s.m. A year of exemption from assessment (on some special account)
To seek pardon or forgiveness, to beg pardon; to apologize
s.m. A deed of immunity or exemption from taxes, &c.; a deed of amnesty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُعَافِی (mu'āfī).

def: s.f. pl. of mǒʻāfī, q.v.

ety: for A. معاهدة mǒʻāhadat, inf. n. of عاهد 'to make a compact, or covenant, &c. with,' iii of عہد 'to make a compact,' &c.
def: s.m. Entering into a compact, or confederacy, or alliance, &c. (with); compact, contract, covenant, agreement, confederacy, alliance, league, treaty, engagement.
more: (inf. III. of عہد) s. m. Confederacy, confederation, alliance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُعَاهَدَه (mu'āhada), borrowed from Arabic مُعَاهَدَة (muʕāhada), from عَاهَدَ (ʕāhada).

ety: for A. معاقبة, inf. n. of عاقب 'to punish,' iii of عقب 'to come after,' &c.
def: s.f. Punishing, chastising; punishment, chastisement; penalty.
more: (inf. III. of عقب Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. Punishment.

ety: for A. معالجة mǒʻālajat, inf. n. of عالج 'to treat medically, to apply a remedy to,' &c., iii of علج 'to become strong,' &c.
def: s.m. Treating medically; applying a remedy (to), healing, curing; — adjusting, or managing (an affair); medical treatment; a remedy; — adjustment (of an affair); — mǒʻālaja karnā (-kā), To treat medically, to apply a remedy (to), to heal, cure.
more: n. m. Medical treatment ( ilāj , 3). muālijā k. , v. a. To attend, treat, as a physician ( ilāj k . 1).
(inf. III. of علج) s. m. 1. Curing, healing, treatment. 2. A remedy. معالجه کرنا muʼāﻌlaja karnā, To heal, cure, attend (as a physician).

ety: act. part. of عالج; see next
def: s.m. One who treats patients (whether sick or wounded) medically or curatively, a physician.
more: n. m. علج A physician; doctor.
(part. act. III. of علج) s. m. A physician.

ety: for A. معاملة mǒʻāmalat, inf. n. of عامل 'to transact business with; to deal with in buying and selling,' iii of عمل 'to work,' &c.
def: s.m. Transacting business (with), dealing (with), trading, or bargaining (with); — dealing, transaction, negotiation, business, commerce, traffic; bargain; contract; correspondence; — sexual intercourse; — proceeding, procedure; behaviour; — affair, matter, concern; particular; — cause, or suit (in law), a case; — dominion, jurisdiction
more: n. m. عمل acted. See بيوﮩـار 1, 2, & امر (1, 2). 1. Dealing. 2. Negotiation. 3. A contract; bargain. 4. An affair; matter; concern. 5. Proceeding; particular. 6. Cause or suit in law; a case. 7. A lass. 8. Sexual intercourse. muāmala-bāzārī meṅ , In course of business. muāmalah bannā , v. n. 1. To be settled (a business); struck (a bargain). 2. To gain one's end. muāmala pukht-paz yā pakkā k . To settle a matter; conclude a bargain. muāmala-pardāzī k . To act. muāmala paṛnā , v. n. To have to do or deal with. muāmala khārijī , n. m. An irrelevant matter. muāmala-dāṅ, muāmala-shinās , n. m. A man of business. muāmala-dānī , n. f. A knowledge of business. muāmala saṅgīn , n. m. A grave matter; a serious business. muāmala-i-fāsid , n. m. A dishonest transaction. muāmale kā sachchā, khush-muāmala , An honest person. muāmale kā khoṭā , A dishonest person. muāmala k. , v. a. 1. To deal with; t…

s. m. (inf. III. of عمل Heb. and Aram. ) Treaty, affair, business, negotiation, transaction, behaviour, procedure, concern, cause or suit in law. (Plur. معاملات muʼāﻌmalāt ). معامله رس muʼāﻌmala-ras, adj. Comprehending the affair, the transaction or the suit. معامله رسي muʼāﻌmala-ras-ī, s. f. The comprehension of the affair or suit, &c. معامله کرنا muʼāﻌmala karnā, To deal with, treat with, negotiate.
subs: A matter to be settled, or to succeed; a bargain to be struck; an end to be gained
or mǒʻāmala pukht-paz karnā, To settle a matter finally or definitely; to conclude a bargain
or mǒʻāmala mukammal honā, A transaction, or a bargain, to be completed
s.f. The performing (or performance) of a business or affair; — the transacting (or transaction) of business
To transact (or to carry on) business (for), to act (for)
v.n. Dealings to take place (between); to have to do or deal (with)
or mǒʻāmala-shinās, adj. & s.m. Well acquainted with business, &c.; — a business-man

adj. & s.m. Thoroughly comprehending an affair, or a transaction, or a suit; — one who is conversant with any matter, or who thoroughly comprehends and understands an affair; one who penetrates to the bottom of an affair
s.f. Penetrating to the bottom of an affair, &c.; thorough comprehension of an affair, or business, or suit
s.m. A serious business, a grave matter
s.m. A corrupt or dishonest transaction
or ḵẖẉush-mǒʻāmala, adj. & s.m. True, or straightforward, or honest in (his) dealings or transactions; — an honest man
adj. & s.m. Fraudulent or dishonest in business or in (his) dealings; — a dishonest man
(-se), To carry on business transactions (with), to trade (with), to buy and sell (with), to deal (with); to contract, to bargain; to treat (with), to negotiate (with)
The true or real facts of an affair, or of a case; the merits of a case
adv. At the time of the transaction
s.f. Ground or cause of action
ex: حکومت نے اس معاملے کو نظر انداز کیا ہے
ḥukūmat ne is mu'āmle ko naẓar andāz kiyā hai
The government has ignored this matter.
wety: borrowed from Classical Persian مُعَامَلَه (mu'āmala), from Arabic مُعَامَلَة (muʕāmala), from the root ع م ل (ʕ m l) related to work and deeds.

ety: fr. mǒʻāmala
def: s.f. Dealing, transacting business; transaction, negotiation, &c. (i.q. mǒʻāmala).

ety: for A. معانقة mǒʻānaqat, inf. n. of عانق 'to embrace (a person
def: s.m. Throwing the arms round the neck (of another), embracing, hugging; — an embrace.
more: (inf. III. of عنق Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Throwing the arms round anotherʼs neck, embracing.

ety: act. part. of عاند 'to act with opposition, disobedience, or rebellion,' iii of عند 'to turn aside; to exceed bounds in disobedience,' &c.

more: (part. act. III. of عند) adj. Perverse, refractory, obstinate, stubborn.

def: adj. ( Gram .) Feminine.
more: (part. pass. II. of أنث Heb. ) adj. Feminine, effeminate.

more: (inf. III. of عرض) s. m. Contradiction, opposition; antithesis.

def: adj. & s.m. Opposing; adverse; — an opponent, adversary, a competitor.
more: (part. act. III. of عرض) 1. adj. Opposing, adverse. 2. s. m. An adversary, opponent, competitor.

more: (inf. III. of عشر) s. f. Living and eating together.

more: (part. act. III. of عصر Heb. ) adj. Contemporary. (Plur. معاصرين muʼāﻌṣirīn ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُعَاصِر (mu'āsir), borrowed from Arabic مُعَاصِر (muʕāṣir), from عَاصَرَ (ʕāṣara).

ety: for A. معاتبة, inf. n. of عاتب 'to reprove,' &c.; — see mǒʻātib
def: s.f. Reproving, blaming, chiding, &c.; reproof, blame, censure, reprehension, expostulation.

ety: act. part. of عاتب, iii and trans. of عتب 'to reprove, blame, censure'

ety: for A. معاودة, inf. n. of عاود 'to return again and again (to a thing, an act, or a place
def: s.f. Returning (to a former habitation, or habit, or action); coming and going, coming (or going) to and fro; — relapsing; a relapse.
more: (inf. III. of عود Heb. ) s. f. Returning (to a former habitation); coming or going.

def: s.f. Aiding, assisting; aid, assistance, help, succour
more: n. f. Assistance; help; aid ( sahāī ). muāvanat k. , v. a. To help; assist.
(inf. III. of عون) s. f. Assistance, help, aid.

s. m. (inf. III. of عوض) Retaliating, returning like for like, substitution, exchange.

r: mǒʻāwaẓat, mǒʻāwaẓa, mǒʻāwaza dilānā, ko, mǒʻāwaẓa-ě-naqdī

more: s. f. (inf. III. of عوض) Retaliating, returning like for like, substitution, exchange.

more: n. m. 1. A helper; supporter. 2. An assistant. muāvin-i-jurm , An accessory; abetter. muāvin-i-daryā ; H. sahāyak-naddī , ( Geog .) A tributary.
(part. act. III. of عون) adj. Assisting, auxiliary; (subst.) An assistant.

ety: for A. معاينة 'to see, view, or meet, face to face,' iii of عان (for عين

more: (inf. III. of عين Heb. ) s. m. Beholding, seeing clearly or face to face.
subs: v.t. To see, behold, view, look at, inspect; to look into, to examine.

ety: Pārsī mobad; Pehl. mānpat; Zend nmānopaiti (nmāna+paiti), rt. man with ni = S. नि+मन्
def: s.m. A worshipper of the sun, one of the Magi, or Pārsīs, or Guebres; a priest of the Guebres; — a doctor, philosopher, any man of great wisdom whose sayings are quoted; — a vizier, a counsellor of state.
more: s. m. 1. A doctor, philosopher, a counsellor of state. 2. A worshipper of the sun, a priest of the gabrs.

def: s.m. The plantain tree, and fruit, Musa sapientum; — the tree Moringa pterygospermum, or Hyperanthera morunga
subs: s.m. The resin of Gossampinus ramphii; — the gum of the silk-cotton tree.

def: s.f. corr. of moćh, or mūćh, q.v.
more: n. m. H. muṛnā to twist. A sprain; twist. Pāoṅ moch, hātan māṅ chhāle, sās kahe 'chal khet kaṭā le!' My hands are all blistered, my foot has a sprain, My mother-in-law bids me 'go, cut the grain.' moch ānā , v. n. To be sprained.

ety: prob. fr. moćnā, q.v.
def: s.m. A strain, a sprain, a twist; — nib, or point (of anything); — moć ānā (-ko), To receive a sprain, to be sprained.
s.f. The plantain, Musa sapientum; — the silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum.
more: n. m. 1. A plantain tree (?); musasapientum , Wat. 2. The new leaf or fruit springing from the centre.
s. m. A large lump of flesh.
s. m. A plantain tree. (Musa sapientum).

ety: S. मोचकं
def: s.m. The plantain (i.q. moć); — the new leaf, or the fruit, springing from the centre of the plantain tree; — a large lump of flesh; — crops beaten down by wind and rain.

s.m. A liberator; — one who has abandoned all worldly passion and desire, an ascetic, a devotee; — the plantain (i.q. moć, and moćā); — the tree Moringa pterygospermum (i.q. moć); — the silk-cotton tree (syn. semal).

ety: ˚an = in = S. इनी
def: s.f. The wife of a moćī, a shoemaker or cobbler's wife.
s.m. corr. of موشن moshaṇ, q.v. s.m. = S موشا मोषा moshā, s.f. Stealing, pilfering, filching; theft, robbery; plundering, sacking; — swindling, defrauding; embezzling.
more: n. m. S. मोचन Pālī, mochanam . Release ( chhuṭkārā , 1).
s. m. Release, acquittal. ¶ mochnā, v. a. To let go, to set free, to shed, to put off, to extinguish.
s. f. The fem. of موچي mochī, q. v. A saddlerʼs or shoemakerʼs wife.

ety: rt. muć
def: s.m. Releasing, setting free, liberating; loosing, letting loose, discharging; release, liberation, deliverance, acquittal, discharge, dismissal; forgiveness.
s.m. The pith or heart (or the fruit) of the banana tree; — a kind of pungent seed, Nigella indica.

ety: Prk. मोच्छो; S. मोक्षः
def: s.m. = moksh, q.v. s.m. Emancipation, deliverance, freedom, liberation, escape, release; — release from worldly existence, future emancipation, final beatitude (resulting from the soul's final liberation and its exemption from further transmigration); — the act of loosing, loosening, untying, unbinding, undoing, solving, &c.; — acquittance of an obligation
subs: s.m. The way of emancipation or salvation; — the status of emancipation or salvation
s.m. Knowledge of the beatitude attained through final emancipation
s.m. One who has knowledge of the beatitude attained through final emancipation
(fem. -dātrī), or moksh-dāyak (fem. -dāyikā), or moksh-dāyī (fem. -dāyinī), adj. & s.m. Imparting, or causing, emancipation; — an emancipator, a deliverer, a saviour
s.m. Law or rule of emancipation.

ety: S. मोचिकः, rt. मुच्
def: s.m. Name of a caste who are workers in leather; — an individual of that caste; a worker in leather; a saddler; harness-maker; shoemaker, cobbler
more: n. f. 1. A saddler; harness-maker. 2. A shoe-maker; cobbler; cordwainer. Phir bhī mochī ke mochī hī rahe . Prov. A cobbler still he is. mochī-patr, mochī kā sikkā, mochrī, panhaī , n. m. A shoe.

s. m. A saddler, a shoemaker, a cobbler. ¶ (In the Dakhan) mūchī or mūchī-wālā (is said to denote) A cast of people whose profession is painting, bookbinding, making saddles, pālkī- bedding, caps, &c. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m., or moćī-kā sikkā, A shoe (i.q. moćrī).

def: s.f. A kind of prickly night-shade.

1. s. m. Pincers, tweezers. 2. v. a. To pinch. 3. To shut, to close.

s.f. Small forceps, &c. (dim. of moćnā, q.v.).
more: s. f. (dakh.) Shoemakerʼs or saddlerʼs wife.

s.m. See s.v. S. moć.
more: n. m. S. मोचरस The gum of the semal tree; Bombax heptaphyllum . (?)
s. m. Gum of the semal tree. (Bombax heptaphyllum).

more: Poet. n. f. A shoe ( jūtī , 1).

more: n. m. 1. Joy; pleasure. 2. Songs of joy.

more: s. m. Name of a sweetmeat.

more: n. m. S. मुग्ध, , Pr. muddho foolish. A simpleton ( buddhū ).

more: n. m. S. मोदक sweetmeat, or A مدعیٰ provision. 1. A grocer. 2. A corn-chandler. 3. A steward. modī-khānā, modhī-khānā , n. m. A storehouse; godown.
(part. pass. II. of أدي) adj. Performed, paid. ¶ muʼaddī, part. act. Paying, performing what is due; s. f. Cause, motive, causing.

s.m. One whose knowledge is useless or unavailing; one who cultivates any but religious wisdom.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A mallet, pounder, rammer; a washerman's mallet (for beating clothes); a ramrod; the dog-head nail of the cock of a flint-lock; — the double jasmine, Jasminum zambac, var. γ (called also Tuscan jasmine, and 'the double Moogrie').
more: n. m. S. मुद्गर Pr. muggaro . 1. (Dim. mogrī ) A mallet; rammer. 1. Telan sū kahā dhoban ghāṭ? vā ke mogrā, vā ke lāṭh . Prov. The woman who sells oil than laundress is no less, Prov. This mallet works, that club's might doth express. 2. Lete hāṛe khāë ḍogrā, māṅgat māre sīs mogrā . Rus. When the Dogrā wants a loan he's on his knees, For payment ask, he 'll club you if you please. 2. A double jasmine.
s. m. 1. A mallet, a pounder, a rammer. 2. Name of a flower, great double Arabian or Tuscan jasmine. (Jasminum zambac, var. γ : Roxb. Mogorium: Lamarck ).

n. m. S. मुद्ग Pr. muggo a kidney bean. The siliqua of the radish ( sīṅgrī , 1).

ety: Prk. मोग्गरिआ; S. मुद्गर+इका
def: s.f. A small mallet (such as is used to beat even the floors and roofs of houses; and, by washermen, to beat clothes).
more: s. f. A mallet for beating cloths with, &c.

def: s.m. (and f. in H.), Loss of consciousness, fainting, unconsciousness, insensibility; stupefaction; bewilderment, perplexity, distraction; ignorance, folly, fatuity, infatuation, delusion; — fascination, allurement, charm; — error, mistake, going astray; — affection, love, kindness, sympathy, compassion, pity; — (in philos.) darkness or delusion of mind preventing the discernment of truth (leading men to believe in the reality of worldly objects, and to addict themselves to mundane or sensual enjoyment)
more: n. m. S. मोह A. S. myne . 1. Fascination; charm; allurement. 2. Affection; love. moh jānā, mohit h. , v. n. To be fascinated, charmed; to be in love. moh-rūpī , adj. Illusive; delusive. Moh-rūpī sansār. moh k. , v. n. To love; hold dear.

s. f. 1. Fainting, senselessness. 2. Pity, compassion, sympathy, kindness, affection, allurement, fascination, love. موه لينا moh lenā, To attach, allure, fascinate, charm, captivate. موه مين آنا moh meṅ ānā, To faint, as at the sudden appearance of a friend or mistress.
subs: (˚ha+ap˚), s.m. The destroyer of ignorance, or error, &c. (an epithet of Śiva, and of Wisdom)

v.n. To become unconscious, to faint or swoon (as at the sudden appearance of a friend or mistress).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Stupefying, depriving of consciousness or sensation; perplexing, bewildering; deceiving, deluding, leading astray, alluring, seducing; infatuating; fascinating, captivating, charming, winning; — one who fascinates, or charms, &c.; — an enchanter; a sweetheart, a lover; — an epithet of Krishṇ; a proper name; — name of one of the five arrows of Kām-dev; — the overpowering of reason and reflection by worldly or sensual pleasure; delusion, infatuation; temptation, seduction; — sexual intercourse
more: 1. s. m. A sweetheart, fascination; a name of Kr̤ish̤ṇa. 2. adj. Fascinating, charming, depriving of sense, captivating. موهن بهوگ mohan-bhog, A kind of sweetmeat. موهن مالا mohan-mālā, Name of an ornament, a string or necklace composed of gold beads and corals.
subs: s.m. A particular kind of sweetmeat (the being subject to an infatuation)
s.m. The beloved Mohan, an epithet of Krishṇ
s.m. A string, or necklace, of corals and gold beads (or of gold beads, corals, pearls, and rudrāksh).

def: n. m. 1. A sweetheart. 2. An epithet of Krishnā. mohan-bhog , n. m. A sweetmeat ( halvā , 1). mohan-mālā , n. f. A necklace of gold, pearls, coral, and rudrāksh , a. v.

more: 1. s. f. An enchantress. 2. Fascination, a charm, a philter. 3. adj. fem. Fascinating, depriving of the powers of reflection.

ety: pass. part. of احکم 'to make or render firm or strong,' &c.; iv of حکم 'to prevent, restrain,' &c.

Made (or rendered) firm, &c.; firm, stable, strong, solid, compact, &c.; robust; tight; fortified, strengthened; — (a passage of the Qorʼān, &c.), of which the meaning is secured from change or alteration; not abrogated; which still holds good; — incontrovertible; clear, perspicuous; unambiguous, unequivocal; — adv. Firmly, strongly, strenuously; tightly, &c.
more: adj. حکم Strengthened; firm. mohkam k. , v. a. To strengthen; fortify.
(part. pass. IV. of حکم Heb. ) adj. Strengthened, firm, fortified, strong. adv. Strenuously, firmly.
subs: v.t. To render firm, or stable, or strong, &c.; to strengthen; to fortify.

ety: fr. A. mǒḥkam
def: s.f. Firmness, stability, strength, solidity, compactness, &c.

subs: (-ko), To grant a respite (to); to allow time (for); to delay, defer, postpone

s.f. Reasonable or fitting delay, or time, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُهْلَت (muhlat), borrowed from Arabic مُهْلَة (muhla), from مَهَلَ (mahala). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi مہلت (mhlt /⁠muhlat⁠/).

def: n. f. مـﮩل See فراغت 1. Delay; respite. 2. Intermission; cessation; armistice. 3. Time; leisure. mohlat denā , v. a. To allow time; defer or postpone. mohlat milnā , v. n. To be allowed time or a respite. mohlat-i-munāsib , G. G. Reasonable time.
more: (from مـﮩل Heb. and Chald. ) s. f. 1. Retarding, deferring. 2. Delay, laziness, indolence, respite, time, leisure, intermission, cessation, armistice.

more: (part. act. IV. of هلک Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Destroying, killing, fatal, deadly, mortal, destructive.

ety: moh˚ = Prk. मोह(इ), or मोहे(इ) = S. मोहय(ति), caus. of rt. मुह्
def: v.t. To allure, tempt, seduce; to infatuate, to delude; to fascinate, enchant, charm
more: 1. v. a. To fascinate, to enchant, charm, allure, delude. 2. adj. Fascinating, captivating, charming, winning.

def: n. a. To fascinate; charm; enchant.

adj. Charming; fascinating; winning. Beṭī kī zāt mohnī hai . Prov. A daughter's an enchantress gay. mohnī , n. f. 1. Fascination. 2. A charm; philter. Āṅkh laṛte hī ho gaī āshiq, Mohnī thī muë ke kājal meṅ . Wom. Jān Sāhab . The wretch! he wantoned with his eyes and lit a flame, The lamp-black in his eyes a witching spell became. 3. An enchantress; a charmer; a fascinating woman. 3. A fille de joie .

n. f. 1. A seal. 2. The impression of a seal; a stamp. 3. A gold coin; gold mohar ( ashrafī ). mohr'eṅ , n. m. ( Arith .) The rule of Practice. Mohreṅ sīkhtā hai . mohr-bar-dār , n. m. Keeper of the seal. mohr-i-taqlīdī , G. G. A counterfeit seal. mohr-i-hākim, mohr-mansabī , n. f. An official seal. mohr-i-khās, yā dastī , n. f. A signet. mohr-i-shāhī , n. f. The privy seal; royal signet. mohr k. yā lagānā , v. a. To stamp; seal. mohr-kan , n. m. A seal engraver; a lapidary. mohr-kanī , n. f. Seal-engraving. mohr lagānā , v. a. 1. To seal up; put a seal on. 2. To close; shut. moh'rī , adj. 1. Sealed; sealed up. 2. Bearing one's signet. mohrī ruqqā , n. m. A note of hand bearing one's signet, or stamped with one's seal.
more: n. m. A marriage portion or gift settled by the husband upon the wife before marriage; jointure; alimony. mahr-bāṅdhnā , v. a. To settle a portion on a wife. mahr bakhshnā , v. n. To give up a jointure. mahr kā dāvā k. , v. a. To claim a marriage portion. mahr-āna , n. m. The Qāzi's marriage fee.
n. f. 1. Kindness; favor. Bhagvān ne ab to mehr kar dī 2. Feeling; sympathy ( dard , 2). Iske jī meṅ zarā mehr nahīṅ . 3. Prosperity ( lahr bahar ).
(Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. 1. A marriage portion or gift settled upon the wife before marriage, alimony, jointure. 2. Contracting by writing for such settlement.

Love, friendship, affection, kindness. مـﮩرانگيز mihr-angez, Love-exciting, friendly. مـﮩرکش mihr-kash, adj. Loving, affectionate. مـﮩرور mihr-war, adj. Friendly, kind. مـﮩرورزي mihr-warz-ī, s. f. Exercise or practice of friendship. 2. (Sans. मिहिर ) s. m. The sun. 3. The name of a Persian month, in which falls the autumnal equinox.

def: s.m. = مہرا muhrā or mǒhrā, q.v. s.m. The front; van, vanguard (syn. agārī; harāwal)
subs: adv. In the front; in the van.

def: n. m. 1. Any natural protuberance of the bone, as in the neck-joint; the ankle joint in a man or horse; backbone; vertebra. 2. A shell. 3. A shell, piece of wood, etc. for polishing or calendering. 4. Gloss; polish. 5. A chess-man; a man (in draughts). Dast mohrā, khāna pur . (Chess-players). The hand on the piece, the square is filled. mohrā k. , v. a. To polish. mohre-dār , adj. Polished; smooth.
more: n. m. H. muṅh mouth. The front ( āgā , 1, 2). mohre par , adj. In front. mohre par rakhnā , v. a. To put in front.

n. m. mahrī , n. f. 1. An effeminate person. 2. ( mahtā ) A kahār so-called from respect or courtesy. So called from mehra one who lives among women, because kahārs have access to the women's apartments.
(part. pass. II. of هرا) adj. Pounded, comminuted.

def: n. f. S. मुख H. muṅh mouth. 1. The ends of a sleeve or a pair of trousers; the bore of a gun, etc. 2. The head-stall.
more: 1. (corrupt. of موري) s. f. A drain, a gutter, aqueduct, conduit. 2. (from مـﮩر muhr ) adj. Sealed. 3. mihr-ī, s. f. (1. in compos.) Friendship. (2.) A kind of guitar or harp.
s. f. The cuff of the sleeve of a jacket, or the extremity of the leg of a pair of trousers, the bore (of a gun, &c.).

ety: act. part. of احرق 'to burn,' iv of حرق 'to be burnt,' &c.
def: adj. Burning; inflammatory; ardent; warmth-giving.

ety: act. part. of احرك, iv of حرك, and syn. with حرّك
def: part. adj. & s.m. Moving, putting in motion; stirring, motory, stimulant, incentive; — marking a consonant with a vowel; — mover, stirrer up, inciter, instigator, exciter (of); adviser, persuader, tempter; encourager, promoter.

ety: acc. of mǒḥriq
def: adv. In a burning state.

ety: act. part. of احرم 'to enter upon a thing (or state, &c.
def: part. adj. Entering on the sacred month Muḥarram; engaged in the sacred pilgrimage to Mecca; performing the ceremonies enjoined on entering a sacred place.

s.m. See s.v. مہر muhr or mǒhr. s.f. A seal; seal-ring; — impression of a seal; a stamp; — a gold coin (current in India for about ₤1 16s.), a gold-mohur; — a cautery, or the instrument which makes the impression; — maidenhood, virginity; — mǒhr-bar-dār, s.m. Keeper of the seal
subs: or mǒhr-ě-manṣabī, s.f. An official seal
or mǒhr-ě-dastī, s.f. A private seal; a signet
s.m. Holder or keeper of the seal
or mǒhr lagānā (-meṅ, or par?), To put a seal (on), to seal; to stamp
(-měṅ), To put a seal (on); to seal up; — to close, shut.

ety: pass. part. of احصن 'to render inaccessible; to protect; to fortify,' &c., iv of حصن 'to be inaccessible,' &c.
def: part. Guarded, preserved, protected; — chaste, continent.
more: (part. pass. IV. of حصن) adj. Chaste, continent.

ety: for A. محصنة mǒḥṣanat, fem. of mǒḥṣan
def: adj. = mǒḥṣan.

ety: act. part. of احسن 'to do good, to act well; to benefit,' iv of حَسُنَ 'to be good, or comely,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Benefiting; obliging; beneficent, generous; — one who does good, or who is the causer of good, or of what is comely, &c.; one who obliges; a benefactor, a patron
more: n. m. A benefactor; patron. mohsin-kush , adj. Ungrateful.
(part. act. IV. of حسن) adj. Benefiting, obliging; (subst.) A benefactor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُحْسِن (muhsin), from Arabic مُحْسِن (muḥsin).

ety: act. part. of اِهْتدي 'to be guided aright,' &c., viii of هدي 'to guide aright,' &c.
def: part. adj. Guided aright, directed or led (into the way of salvation); directed, guided, or steered (to the right or left).

ety: pass. part. of احتاج, viii of (and syn. with
def: adj. Necessitous, needy, indigent, poor, in want, in need (of); — defective, wanting; — s.m. One who is in need, a poor man, a pauper; a beggar; — a cripple
more: adj. 1. Poor; indigent; necessitous; needy. 2. Defective; wanting. mohtāj , n. m. 1. A beggar; pauper. 2. A cripple. mohtāj-khānah . A poor house. mohtāj k. , v. a. To impoverish; make poor. Khudā kisī ko kisī kā mauhtāj na kare! Prov. May God never make any man dependant upon another. mohtāj h. , v. n. 1. To have occasion for; to be in need of. 2. To be very poor. mohtāj'gī, mohtājī , n. f. Indigence; poverty; want of means.
(part. pass. VIII. of حوج Heb. ) adj. Necessitous, indigent, in want.
subs: (-ko), To reduce to need or want, to make poor, to impoverish
(-kā), To be (or to stand) in need (of), to need; to be poor; to have occasion (for).

ety: fr. moḥtāj
def: s.f. Need, want, want of means, indigence, poverty.

ety: act. part. of احتکر, viii of (and syn. with
def: s.m. One who buys (grain, &c.) and withholds it in order that it may become scarce and dear; a forestaller, a regrater.

ety: pass. part. of احتال, viii of (and syn. with
def: adj. & s.m. Artful, crafty, subtle, insidious; — an artful or crafty person; a deceiver; — (in Law) a person who accepts the responsibility of one person for his claim upon another
more: (part. pass. VIII. of حول) adj. Insidious; deceiver.
subs: The person to whom the responsibility for a debt or claim was transferred.

ety: act. part. of احتلم, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Dreaming (esp. of copulation); experiencing an emission of the seminal fluid in dreaming, having a wet dream; polluted; — arrived at the age of puberty; — a dreamer; one who has a wet dream; one arrived at the age of puberty.

ety: act. part. of احتمل, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Taking up, bearing, enduring; patient, long-suffering; — suspected, conjectured; — suspicious, doubtful, ambiguous; — admitting (of), capable (of), susceptible (of); liable (to); — admissible, allowable, possible, probable; — s.m. A taker up (of a burden), bearer, sustainer; endurer, sufferer; one who takes upon himself
more: adj. حمل bore. 1. Suspected; doubtful; ambiguous. 2. Susceptible; liable. 3. Possible; probable.
(part. act. VIII. of حمل) adj. 1. Suspected, conjectured. 2. Suspicious, doubtful, ambiguous, possible, probable, suffering patiently.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Suspected, conjectured.

def: n. m. A manager; superintendent; an inspector; overseer. mohtamim-i-akhbār , n. m. An editor of a newspaper. mohtamim-i-band o bast , n. m. A settlement officer. mohtamim-i-matba , A press manager.
more: (rather مـﮩتم muhtamm, part. act. VIII. of همّ) Taking on oneʼs self the concerns of others; (subst.) Commissioner, agent, trustee, inspector, superintendent.

ety: for mǒhtammuṉ, act. part. of اِهْتمّ 'to be anxious (about
def: part. adj. & s.m. Anxious, or concerned (about); taking upon oneself the concerns of others; — a commissioner, an agent, a manager, trustee, superintendent, inspector, overseer
subs: s.m. Manager of a printing press.

def: s.f. Agency, managership, &c. (see mǒhtamim).
n. f. Managership (اﮩتمام 1, 2).

ety: pass. part. of احترم 'to hold in reverence or honour; to reverence,' &c., viii of حرم 'to be entitled to reverence,' &c.
def: part. Revered, respected, honoured, venerable, sacred.
more: (part. act. VIII. of حرم Heb. ) adj. Honoured, respected, sacred, revered, venerable, estimable.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُحْتَرَم (muhtaram), in turn borrowed from Arabic مُحْتَرَم (muḥtaram), passive participle of اِحْتَرَمَ (iḥtarama), from the root ح ر م (ḥ r m).

ety: pass. part. of احتشم 'to be master of many dependents and servants,' viii of حشم, but prob. formed fr. ḥasham, q.v.
def: part. adj. Attended by many followers or dependents, &c.; powerful, great.
more: (part. act. VIII. of حشم) adj. Having many followers, powerful, great.

ety: act. part. of احتسب 'to reckon upon a reward'; 'to include (a thing
def: s.m. A reckoner, calculator; — the inspector of the markets, and of the weights and measures, &c.; the superintendent of the police, who examines weights, &c., and prohibits unlawful games, drinking, and the like; a censor.
more: n. m. حسب calculating. A censor. A superintendant of market and police; and police; an officer appointed to take cognizance of improper behaviour, as of indecency, drunkenness, gambling, and of the sale of intoxicating drugs and liquors, and false weights and measures.
(part. act. VIII. of حسب) s. m. 1. A censor. 2. Superintendent of weights, measures, and provisions.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian محتسب (muhtasib), from Arabic مُحْتَسِب (muḥtasib), active participle of اِحْتَسَبَ (iḥtasaba).

ety: fr. moḥtasib
def: s.f. The duty or office of Mǒḥtasib; administration of the police.

ety: pass. part. of احتاط 'to surround, encompass,' &c., viii of حاط (for حوط
def: part. Surrounded, encompassed; besieged; — guarded; circumspect.
more: (part. pass. VIII. of حوط) adj. Guarded, circumspect.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُحْتَاط (muhtāt), borrowed from Arabic مُحْتَاط (muḥtāṭ).

ety: act. part. of اهتال 'to be terrified,' &c., viii of هال (for هول
def: part. adj. Terrified, afraid; trembling.
adj. A dialec. corr. of mohī, q.v. (Braj), 1˚ pron. obl., see s.v. mo; — 2˚ = mohit, q.v. pron. obl. (Braj, or dialec.) Me (i.q. mujh); — of me, my (i.q. merā)
subs: On or upon me (i.q. mujh-par); — by me, in my possession (i.q. mere-pās)
moro, morau, morauṅ, Of me, my, mine (i.q. merā)

Like me (i.q. mujh-sā, or mere samān)
Of me, my, mine (i.q. merā; see kar)

To me; me (i.q. mujhe); — mo-hi, or mo-hī, Me indeed; to me truly, &c. (i.q. mujh-hī).

def: s.f. = H موٴيا मोइया moʼiyā, s.m. (dialec.) A kind of powder, made of poppy-heads, half-parched aniseed, and sugar (administered in cases of acute dysentery).
s.m. A skein (of thread).
n. m. 1. A skein of thread. 2. (Hin. moī , n. f.) A powder of poppy heads, half parched aniseed, and sugar, given in acute dysentery. moiyā sā peṭ , Wom. A smooth, soft belly.
s.m. = H موئی मोई moʼī, s.f. (dialec.) A kind of powder, made of poppy-heads, half-parched aniseed, and sugar (administered in cases of acute dysentery).
1˚ = mūj, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of mauj, q.v.; — 3˚ for mojā, corr. of moza, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَوْج (mawj).

def: s.m. corr. of موزه moza, q.v. s.m. A stocking; — a boot; — a glove
subs: adj. & s.m. Biting (or a horse that bites) at the foot or leg of his rider.

ety: act. part. of اعجز 'to render powerless or impotent; to disable (an opponent
def: s.m. A miracle (i.q. mǒʻjiza, the com. form, q.v.; lit. 'that by which a prophet disables the opponent in a contest')

ety: for A. معجزة mǒʻjizat, intens. of mǒʻjiz, q.v.
def: s.m. A miracle (performed by a prophet, in contradistinction to karāmat, which is a miracle performed by a saint, or righteous man not claiming to be a prophet)
subs: To perform a miracle or miracles
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُعْجِزَه (mu'jiza), borrowed from Arabic مُعْجِزَة (muʕjiza). First attested in c. 1697 as Middle Hindi معجزہ (m'jzh).

def: s.m. pl. (of معجزه mǒʻjiza, q.v.), Miracles.
(dialec.) pron. genitive of mo, q.v.

ety: Prk. मोक्खं; S. मुखं
def: s.m. = mokhā, q.v. s.m. An opening, aperture; — peep-hole, spy-hole; air-hole (cf. S. mūshā); a ventilator; — a lattice, window (syn. jharokhā); a sky-light
subs: s.m. Ridge (of a thatch); — topping, or coping (of a wall), &c. (i.q. maṅgrā; magrī, qq.v.).

def: s. m. An airhole, spy-hole, or hole in a wall for light, &c.

ety: Prk. मोक्खअं; S. मुख+कं
def: s.m. An opening, aperture; — peep-hole, spy-hole; air-hole (cf. S. mūshā); a ventilator; — a lattice, window (syn. jharokhā); a sky-light
more: n. m. S. मूष air hole. 1. An aperture; opening. 2. An air-hole; a ventilator. 3. A sky-light. Khol mokhā jo āve jhokā . Open the ventilator, let in the air! (Open your purse strings, relief from pain secure!).
subs: s.m. Ridge (of a thatch); — topping, or coping (of a wall), &c. (i.q. maṅgrā; magrī, qq.v.).

ety: Prk. मोक्कडओ or मोक्कलओ; S. मुक्त+ल+कः, or मोक (rt. मुच्)+ल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Loose, free, unbound, unconfined, unrestrained, at large; — opened, expanded, extended; — open, wide open (as a box, or door, or road, &c.); — loose, separate, distinct, apart; — free (from inconvenience, or distress, &c.); relieved; having the heart expanded; refreshed, cheerful (syn. kushāda); — enough, ample, abundant; — s.m. A plant; a vegetable
more: adj. 1. Open; wide open. Chāroṅ raste mokle . Prov. All four roads are open. 2. Enough ( muktā ).
adj. (dakh.) Open, unrestrained, extensive: see کشاده kushāda.
subs: s.m. (dialec.) A young shoot, a sprout.

def: adj. (dialec.) Much, &c. (i.q. muktā, q.v.; — syn. mukterā). part. (f. -ā), Loosed, let loose, set free, let go; redeemed, released, extricated, liberated, absolved, emancipated (from sin, or from corporeal existence), saved, finally happy; — s.m. A liberated person; — the spirit released from worldly existence and reintegrated with its divine original.

s.m. Emancipation, deliverance, freedom, liberation, escape, release; — release from worldly existence, future emancipation, final beatitude (resulting from the soul's final liberation and its exemption from further transmigration); — the act of loosing, loosening, untying, unbinding, undoing, solving, &c.; — acquittance of an obligation
subs: s.m. The way of emancipation or salvation; — the status of emancipation or salvation
s.m. Knowledge of the beatitude attained through final emancipation
s.m. One who has knowledge of the beatitude attained through final emancipation
(fem. -dātrī), or moksh-dāyak (fem. -dāyikā), or moksh-dāyī (fem. -dāyinī), adj. & s.m. Imparting, or causing, emancipation; — an emancipator, a deliverer, a saviour
s.m. Law or rule of emancipation.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. mokshikā), Setting at liberty, freeing, delivering, emancipating; — one who looses, or unties, or sets free or at liberty; a deliverer, liberator, emancipator, saviour.
s.m. The act of loosing, or loosening; letting go, releasing, setting at liberty, liberation, deliverance, emancipation; exempting from further transmigration.
part. (f. -ā), Set free, let go; allowed to be at large; liberated, emancipated.

ety: Prk. मोल्लं; S. मूल्यं
def: s.m. Purchase; — original value; value, price, worth, purchase-money
more: n. m. S. सूल्य. 1. Purchase. 2. Purchase money; price. 1. Jhūṭe kī kuchh pat nahīṅ, sājan jhūṭ na bol! Lākh-patī kā jhūṭ se do kauḍī ho mol . Prov. 2. Kāre kāre baigan piṭāre bhare jāëṅ, Rājā māṅge mol to dīye na jāëṅ . Wom. Riddle. Ans. Āṅkh . Eyes. mol ans , n. m. A purchased share. mol baṛhānā, yā chaṛhānā , v. a. 1. To enhance or raise the price. 2. To outbid. mol-tol , n. f. Valuation; appraisement. mol denā , v. n. To sell; dispose of. Mol do to ham lete haiṅ. mol ghaṭānā , v. a. To beat down the price. mol lagānā , v. a. To make an offer (for an article); to appraise. mol le-kar chhoṛ denā , v. a. To buy a slave, etc. for the purpose of setting him free. Tum sār ke to woh mol le-kar chhoṛ detā hai . He has bought and liberated many like you. mol lenā ; E. molnā, mulānā , v. a. To buy; purchase. Jā din jūā sīkhiyāṅ sab dukh līnā mol, Kauḍī kharcheṅ gāṅṭh kī saheṅ parāë bol . Rus. W…

s. m. 1. Purchase. 2. Price. مول تول mol-tol, s. m. Fixing a price; traffick, purchasing. مول بڙهانا mol baṛhānā, v. a. To enhance. مول توڙنا mol toṛnā, v. a. (dakh.) To beat down or abate (price). مول ٿهـﮩرانا mol ṭhahrānā, To appraise, to estimate, to value. مول لينا mol-lenā, v. a. To buy, to purchase.
subs: or mol ćaṛhānā (-kā), To enhance, or raise, the price (of); — to bid against (one, at an auction, &c.); to outbid
(-kā), To beat down, or to abate, the price (of)
s.m. Price (indefinitely); price and cost; — fixing the price, or value (of, -kā), valuation, appraisement; — bargaining; purchasing; trading; traffic
or mol-tol ćukānā, or mol-tol karnā (-kā), To fix or settle the price, or value (of), to value, appraise, estimate; to bargain (for)
mol ṭhahrānā or ćukānā or karnā
v.t. 'To give for a price,' to sell (to, -ko)
(-kā), To reduce, or to beat down, the price (of)
(-kā), To value, appraise, &c. (i.q. mol-tol ṭhahrānā); to make an offer (for)
(-ko), To purchase and set free, or to purchase (a slave, &c.) for the purpose of setting (him, or it) at liberty
v.t. 'To take by purchase, or for a price,' to buy, purchase.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀽𑀮 (mūla), from Sanskrit मूल (mūla). Cognate with Bengali মূল (mul), Gujarati મૂળ (mūḷ).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = موڙکا moṛkā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) A sprout, or shoot (of corn, &c.); — external piles (i.q. moṛ, and moṛ-kā āzār).

ety: fr. mol, q.v.
def: v.t. (dialec.) To buy, to purchase (i.q. mol-lenā, q.v.s.v. mol).

ety: prob. = S. मधू+मल
def: s.m. Bees-wax, wax
more: n. m. Wax; bees-wax. Mom, gonauṅdī paṭyā kar le, āṅkhoṅ kar le surmā! Mere sāth tū chal de, piyārī, khaiyo laḍḍū, khurmā! Play. Go wax and gum, and part your hair, and unguents apply! And come with me, my love, on dates and sweets you'll feast for aye! mom , adj. Soft; very soft. mom-jāmā , n. m. Wax-cloth; cerecloth. Halvāīyoṅ kī dhotī mom-jāma ho jātī hai . Prov. The Indian confectioner's small breech to oil-cloth grows. (He wipes his greasy hands on it). mom-dil , adj. Tender-hearted. mom-raugan, mom-rogan , n. m. A polish of wax and oil. mom k. , v. a. 1. To make soft or pliable. 2. To melt; thaw; move. 3. To soften; assuage. mom kī battī , n. f. A wax or composition candle. mom kī nāk , n. f. lit . a nose of wax. A fickle person. Mom kī nāk hai, jidhar chāho udhar moṛ do! He has a nose of wax, you can turn it which way you will.

s. m. Wax. موم بتي mom-battī, s. f. A wax-candle. موم جامه mom-jāma, s. m. Wax-cloth, cerecloth. موم دل mom-dil, adj. Tender-hearted. موم روغن mom-raug̠ẖan, Wax and oil mixed together. موم کي ناک mom kī nāk, A person of a fickle disposition.
subs: or mom-kī battī, s.f. A wax-candle; a composition candle
vulg. mom-rogan, s.m. Wax and oil mixed (com. used for polishing furniture)
s.f. 'A nose of wax'; a person of a fickle disposition
v.t. 'To make or convert into wax'; to make soft or pliable; to soften; to melt, thaw, move
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian موم (mōm, mūm). Compare Bengali মোম (mōm), Punjabi موم (mom).

def: s. (dakh.) Candlestick.

ety: mom, q.v.+ī = Zend aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Waxen; — soft as wax; of the colour of wax; — (applied to chintz) prepared after stamping by covering the flowers with wax to prevent their being spoiled by other colours afterwards applied; sized (chintz; e.g. momī ćhīṅṭ, or simply momī)
more: adj. Waxen; soft as wax. momī chhīṅṭ, momī . Sized chintz. momī motī , A mock pearl of hollow glass filled with wax.
adj. 1. Waxen. 2. Of the colour of wax. 3. (applied to chintz) Prepared after stamping by covering the flowers with wax to prevent their being spoiled by other colours afterwards applied.
subs: s.m. An imitation pearl of glass filled with wax.

ety: act. part. of اعمل 'to cause to do, or work,' &c., iv of عمل 'to do,' &c.
def: part. Causing to do, or work, &c.; causing to be done or performed.

def: s.m. A mummy; — the substance with which mummies are preserved.
adj. Of, or like, a mummy; — s.f. A mummy; — name of a medicine
subs: (-kī), To beat (one) to a mummy, or to a jelly.

1. adj. Of or like a mummy. 2. s. f. Name of a medicine. See موميا momiyā.

Like me (i.q. mujh-sā, or mere samān)
Of me, my, mine (i.q. merā; see kar)
mo-kau, mo-koṅ, mo-ko, mo-kāṅ, mo-kā, To me; me (i.q. mujh-ko; mujhe)
To me; me (i.q. mujhe); — mo-hi, or mo-hī, Me indeed; to me truly, &c. (i.q. mujh-hī).

def: n. m. Dim. muṅḍhiyā , n. m. 1. (H. maṅḍh cover) A high stool made of reeds and rope. Sir par jālī, peṭ se khālī, paslī dekh ek ek nirālī, Mujh ko āve yehī parekh, pair na gardan moṅḍhā ek . Riddle. Ans. Moṅḍhā . Woven his head, in stomach naught, each rib I separate see, Nor legs nor neck he has, a shoulder sole he seems to me. 2. ( muḍḍhā ) The shoulder. 3. A gusset on the shoulder of a native coat. 4. ( mūṅh ) The mouth of a bag. moṅḍhe-vālī , n. f. A prostitute; harlot.

ety: Prk. मुड्ढा or मुंढा; S. मूर्धा, base मूर्धन्
def: s.m. A kind of seat, or stool (made of reeds, or cane, and cord); a footstool; — the shoulder (i.q. muḍḍhā); a hump; a lump; — a gusset on the shoulder of a coat; — mouth (of a bag); — a sprout or shoot (of sugar-cane).
more: s. m. 1. The shoulder, a lump. 2. A stool, a footstool. See موڙها.

def: s.f. = mogrī, q.v. s.f. A small mallet (such as is used to beat even the floors and roofs of houses; and, by washermen, to beat clothes).

s.m. corr. of موه moh, q.v. s.m. (and f. in H.), Loss of consciousness, fainting, unconsciousness, insensibility; stupefaction; bewilderment, perplexity, distraction; ignorance, folly, fatuity, infatuation, delusion; — fascination, allurement, charm; — error, mistake, going astray; — affection, love, kindness, sympathy, compassion, pity; — (in philos.) darkness or delusion of mind preventing the discernment of truth (leading men to believe in the reality of worldly objects, and to addict themselves to mundane or sensual enjoyment)
subs: (˚ha+ap˚), s.m. The destroyer of ignorance, or error, &c. (an epithet of Śiva, and of Wisdom)
s.m. The darkness occasioned by moh, q.v.
(-par ?), To be fascinated (by), be charmed (with), be enamoured (of)
adj. Infatuating; delusive; illusive; — fascinating, charming
(-ko), To allure; to infatuate, &c. (i.q. mohnā, q.v.); — moh karnā (-kā), To feel affection, or compassion, &c. (for); to love
adj. (f. -ī), Abounding in, or under the influence of, infatuation, or delusion, or error, &c.; leading to delusion, delusive; perplexing; false
v.n. To become unconscious, to faint or swoon (as at the sudden appearance of a friend or mistress).

def: s.f. = موري morī, and مہري muhrī, or mǒhrī, qq.v. s.f. A peahen (i.q. mornī, q.v.). s.f. A peahen; — a peg; — pendant (of an ear-ring, or nose-ring).
(from منـﮩ or Pers. موري) s. f. A kennel, a drain.
s.f. (dialec.) Point, tip, end.

s.m. = moṅḍhā, and moṛhā, qq.v. s.m. A kind of seat, or stool (made of reeds, or cane, and cord); a footstool; — the shoulder (i.q. muḍḍhā); a hump; a lump; — a gusset on the shoulder of a coat; — mouth (of a bag); — a sprout or shoot (of sugar-cane).
s.f. = موٿ moṭ, q.v. s.f. (occas. m.), A bundle, parcel, package, bale, load; — gross; amount, total; — a leather bucket (for raising water from a draw-well; — syn. ćaras; purwaṭ)
subs: To sell the whole bundle, or load, &c.; to sell in the lump, or gross; to sell wholesale.

def: adj. = موٿا moṭā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Fat, plump, stout, corpulent; big, bulky; great, large; gross; dense, thick; coarse; inferior; capital (as a letter); material, important; rich, wealthy; — palpable, plain (e.g. moṭī bāt)
subs: To see indistinctly, to be dim-sighted
s.m. A thick reed-pen (for writing capitals with)
A wealthy or well-to-do person; a prize, a catch; — a heavy purse.
